task_completion.description: Verify that the countries shapefile is loaded into the gvSIG project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries' listed in the gvSIG Table of Contents (TOC) on the left side of the screen, or visible as a polygon layer in the map view.

task_completion.description: Verify that the rivers shapefile is loaded into the gvSIG project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'ne_110m_rivers_lake_centerlines' listed in the gvSIG Table of Contents (TOC) or visible as a line layer in the map view.

task_completion.description: Verify that the spatial Intersection geoprocessing tool is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Geoprocessing tool dialog window titled 'Intersection' (or similar spatial overlay tool) open in the gvSIG interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that the rivers and countries layers are selected as the inputs for the intersection operation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the Intersection tool dialog, look for dropdowns or lists where 'ne_110m_rivers_lake_centerlines' and 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries' are selected as the input layer and the overlay/intersect layer.

task_completion.description: Verify that the output file path is correctly specified.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the output file path text box in the Intersection tool dialog. It must show exactly '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/rivers_by_country.shp'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the intersection operation was completed and the new layer is generated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a new layer (e.g., 'rivers_by_country') added to the gvSIG TOC. If the attribute table is visible, it should show combined fields from both inputs (e.g., 'NAME' from countries and 'name' from rivers).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the gvSIG Desktop GUI for the geoprocessing task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show interaction with the gvSIG Desktop application windows, menus, and dialogs, not just a terminal window.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use command-line GIS tools to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that terminal windows do not show commands like 'ogr2ogr', 'geopandas', or 'qgis_process' being used to perform the intersection.

integrity.description: Verify the resulting layer is actually loaded or visible in gvSIG.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final output layer should appear in the gvSIG Table of Contents, indicating it was genuinely created and loaded by the software, rather than just a text file renamed to .shp.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is exactly '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/rivers_by_country.shp'. The input countries layer contains fields: 'NAME', 'ADMIN', 'CONTINENT', and 'POP_EST'. The input rivers layer contains fields: 'name', 'scalerank', and 'featurecla'. A successful intersection will produce a layer whose attribute table contains fields from both of these sets.
